mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand, which translates to higher corporate revenues and profits, driving S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns do not show a statistically significant positive correlation with the month-over-month growth of RSAFS over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant drop in the unemployment rate below a key threshold signals a tightening labor market, indicating strong economic health and boosting investor confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day returns are negative or flat in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE drops below 5.0% for the first time in 6 months.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is low and stable, and the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) is also low, it indicates an accommodative monetary environment, reducing borrowing costs and supporting equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 237.566, FEDFUNDS at 0.14 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 20-day returns are negative when both CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth is below 2% and FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% for more than 40% of such periods.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in housing starts during a post-recession recovery period indicates renewed economic activity and consumer confidence, which typically precedes broader economic growth and positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If, during identified post-recession recovery periods, a >10% MoM increase in HOUST does not lead to positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in at least 70% of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in industrial production reflects strong manufacturing and mining output, indicating a healthy supply side of the economy, which supports corporate earnings and drives S&P 500 returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the 5-day forward returns do not exhibit a positive correlation with the month-over-month percentage change in INDPRO over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of strong payroll employment growth and a declining unemployment rate signifies a robust labor market, leading to increased consumer spending and corporate profits, which positively impacts S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS growth > 0.2% MoM AND UNRATE change < -0.1pp MoM
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 142371.0, UNRATE at 5.1 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: If, when both PAYEMS growth exceeds 0.2% MoM and UNRATE declines by more than 0.1pp MoM, the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 35% of such instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate is at historically low levels and remains stable, it signals an environment of cheap capital, encouraging investment and corporate expansion, which supports equity valuations and leads to positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 40% of periods where FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% and has not changed by more than 0.05% for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive growth in real GDP indicates overall economic expansion and strong aggregate demand, which directly translates to higher corporate earnings and, consequently, positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the quarter-over-quarter annualized growth rate of GDPC1 does not show a statistically significant positive correlation with S&P 500 20-day forward returns over any 10-year rolling window.
